name: Room Connection Graph
description: A topology generator that defines rooms as graph nodes and doorways, corridors, locks, keys, exits, or transitions as validated graph edges before room content is placed.
design_notes: Room connection graphs own solvable topology, not room art placement. A useful graph records graph_node_id, graph_edge_id, entrance and exit nodes, critical_path_node_count, branch_probability, loop_connection_budget, lock/key ordering, graph seed stream, and validation failures before any room templates are instantiated.
design_purpose: Control progression through generated rooms before detailed geometry is placed.
Guarantee reachable starts, goals, keys, locks, shops, secrets, and exits.
Give designers topology knobs such as loops, branches, dead ends, and critical path length.
player_fantasy: The player feels they are exploring a coherent dungeon or facility with routes, loops, and discoveries rather than disconnected rooms.

parameters.name: graph_seed_stream_key
parameters.description: RNG stream used for graph shape so topology can be reproduced independently from room art placement.

parameters.name: graph_node_id
parameters.description: Stable identifier for a room or logical space in the generated graph.

parameters.name: graph_edge_id
parameters.description: Identifier for a connection between two graph nodes.

parameters.name: entrance_node_id
parameters.description: Node that must be reachable before all required progression checks begin.

parameters.name: exit_node_id
parameters.description: Goal node that reachability and critical-path validation must preserve.

parameters.name: critical_path_node_count
parameters.description: Number of nodes on the required route from start to main objective.

parameters.name: branch_probability
parameters.description: Chance or weight for adding optional branches from critical path nodes.

parameters.name: loop_connection_budget
parameters.description: Maximum extra edges that create shortcuts, alternate routes, or cycles.

edge_cases: A lock appears before its key or ability gate, causing a soft-lock.
Doorway sockets selected by room_template_id do not physically align even though the graph edge exists.
Optional branches consume all valid room templates and leave no fallback for the critical path.
Loop connections bypass required tutorial, boss, or reward nodes.
A graph is topologically reachable but no room template exists for a required node tag, size, or socket count.
Graph repair creates a shortcut that makes entrance_node_id or exit_node_id validation pass while lock/key order is still impossible.
A saved run restores visited-room state but not graph_edge_id lock state, reopening or resealing the wrong path.
common_bugs: Connectivity validation checks graph edges but not actual spawned doorway collisions or navmesh reachability.
Random graph traversal uses unordered sets and produces different topology from the same seed.
Dead-end treasure rooms are selected for mandatory objectives because room tags are too broad.
Debug maps show room layout but not graph_edge_id reasons for rejected connections.
Graph node identifiers are regenerated after load, breaking minimap reveal, visited flags, and quest target references.
Cycle repair adds duplicate graph_edge_id values, so one door opens multiple unrelated connections.
balancing_notes: Critical path length drives session duration more reliably than total room count.
Loops reduce backtracking frustration but can weaken lock/key or chase pacing if overused.
Optional branch rewards should match branch risk and distance from the main route.
accessibility_notes: Generated maps should represent graph structure clearly when navigation complexity is high.
Avoid procedurally creating maze-like graphs without landmarks, map tools, or pathfinding support.

implementation_notes.unity: Generate a logical graph first from graph_seed_stream_key, validate lock/key ordering, then instantiate room_template_id prefabs by doorway sockets; draw graph_node_id and graph_edge_id overlays in editor tools.
implementation_notes.godot: Represent topology as data dictionaries or Resources before scene instancing; run reachability and soft-lock validation before adding room scenes to the tree, then serialize graph nodes and edge states for saves.
implementation_notes.unreal: Use DataAssets for room templates and a generator subsystem that builds graph nodes/edges, validates critical path, branches, loops, and lock order, then spawns level instances or PCG content.
implementation_notes.web: Keep the graph as JSON-serializable data with stable node and edge IDs; export failed seeds with graph_seed_stream_key and validation_retries for reproducible bug reports.

relationships.reason: A room connection graph gives room placement route constraints, branch roles, loop budgets, lock order, and reachability validation.
Graph topology needs deterministic RNG streams and serialized node identifiers so bad seeds, saves, and replays reproduce the same routes.
Graph ordering can validate that keys, locks, and critical path gates appear in solvable order.
Optional branches in the graph can host risk-reward rooms without blocking the critical path.

example_games.title: The Binding of Isaac
example_games.note: Uses generated room layouts connected into floor maps.

example_games.title: Dead Cells
example_games.note: Uses generated level routes with branches and exits.
